codeReview.summary: The fix fully addresses the prior VAL-RUNTIME-001 gap: ensure_root_user now has focused runtime regressions for signin success, single signup fallback, and combined dual-failure error context, with only a minimal injectable helper seam added in core/src/auth/mod.rs.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker skill should explicitly cover the case where the required runtime behavior already exists in the incoming dirty tree so workers know whether validation-only completion is acceptable.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The prior failed feature handoff for worker session 650c319f-f200-4db4-a960-6b900c466006 reports that targeted regressions already existed in the pre-existing tree, and its skillFeedback.suggestedChanges asks for guidance on handling already-implemented features.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker skill should document a concrete fallback for proving ensure_root_user behavior by adding a tiny injectable seam when no dedicated auth regression path exists.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The prior fail review flagged missing ensure_root_user runtime coverage, and the fix implements ensure_root_user_with plus three focused tests in core/src/auth/mod.rs, matching the earlier suggestion that the skill needed a fallback procedure for this auth path.

sharedStateObservations.observation: GitNexus project guidance references gitnexus_detect_changes() even though the installed CLI lacks a matching detect_changes command, so workers must infer a fallback from handoff feedback instead of from shared instructions.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The fix handoff records npx gitnexus detect_changes --repo appdb exiting 1 because the subcommand is unavailable, while both CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md still require gitnexus_detect_changes() before commit.

summary: Reviewed the prior fail report, both feature diffs (64d4657 and 3e46b0e plus the formatting-only follow-up d52458c), the fix handoff, and the worker transcript skeleton. The original feature was missing any focused runtime proof for ensure_root_user; the fix adds a minimal test seam and three targeted auth regressions that directly cover the required signin-first, signup-fallback, and dual-error-context behavior, so this review passes.
